ip_outcome_note: Card name is 'Pancham'; depicted figure (small black-and-white panda-like creature with a leaf in its mouth in a bamboo forest) matches the canonical Fighting-type Gen 6 Pokemon, National Pokedex no. 674. Verified via card name, PokemonTCG.io metadata, Bulbapedia: Pancham, and flavor text cross-reference.

trivia_bullets.text: Pancham is National Pokedex no. 674, a Fighting-type Pokemon introduced in Generation VI (X/Y, 2013) in the Kalos region. It evolves into Pangoro at Level 32 — but only if the player has a Dark-type Pokemon in their party at the time of evolution. This party-composition requirement is unique to Pancham in the main series. [Bulbapedia: Pancham; Pokemondb.net; via WebSearch]

The Dark-type party requirement for evolution implies Pancham acquires its Dark type after evolution due to a 'bad influence' — the game design encodes the concept of a naughty child being led astray. Pancham and Pangoro draw inspiration from bancho, the Japanese archetype of juvenile delinquent gang leaders. The leaf in Pancham's mouth is a personality marker: per flavor text, when Pancham makes a big mistake, Pangoro takes the leaf away as discipline. [Bulbapedia: Pangoro design section; WebSearch snippet]

Flavor text on this card: "It follows Pangoro around like a henchman. When Pancham makes a big mistake, its leaf gets taken away." The leaf-as-behavior-detector directly connects to Pangoro's combat mechanic, where Pangoro uses its bamboo leaf to sense minute vibrations and opponent movements. [PokemonTCG.io: sm12-119 flavorText; frontmatter capture]

related_cards.note: Direct evolution; same set; Fighting/Dark type; bamboo-leaf sensory mechanic
